text:
It wasn't any sort of grand adventure. Or secret super-squirrel mission. Just Sarah, a woman busy raising children. One day, Sarah heard her teenage son Dave complaining about a book report due the next morning. Remembering it wasn't exactly her cup of tea—or his favourite movie genre—I suggested we swap. That weekend, Dave brought home an array of superhero comics. The next time I saw Dave, he was heroishly reading one about a quirky, coffee-loving squirrel.

That day wasn't just Dave's birthday. It was our turning page in childhood. I waved goodbye to the hero-comics hero. Dave grinned. I picked up a book. That evening, we were side-by-side in a quiet town bar. Dave's mouth fell open. He picked up his own book.

Over pints of chocolate milk—we hadn't traded books forever—I told him about a small town where a guy wore a red fedora. Dave, deep into his own book, shook his head
